LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NATALIA DIAZ'S MOTION FOR REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF NATALIA DIAZ'S MOTION FOR REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE, WHICH IS UNOPPOSED, IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DIAZ'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS SHALL BE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE, WHEREAS THE REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS SHALL BE DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF SHALL PRESENT AN EXECUTED CIV-110(S), ALONG WITH THIS ORDER, TO THE CLERK. MOVING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATES VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-15-2023 Off Calendar

Motion For Request For Dismissal With Prejudice; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NATALIA DIAZ'S Motion For Request For Dismissal With Prejudice.

Plaintiff Natalia Diaz's Motion For Request For Dismissal With Prejudice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Diaz's individual claims shall be dismissed with prejudice, whereas the representative PAGA claims shall be dismissed without prejudice. Plaintiff shall present an executed CIV-110(s), along with this order, to the Clerk.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeates verbatim the court's ruling and states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
